§6Light Oil§f

§aThe lightest liquid fraction — burn it for high energy or convert it into desh and aromatic hydrocarbons!§f

Light Oil is the third most common refinery product, yielding §e150mB§f per §e1,000mB§f of hot crude oil. Despite its lower volume, it packs a punch with §e1.46 MTU§f and §e2.2 MHE§f per bucket — the highest energy value of the four raw fractions.

§6Production§f:
§b• Oil Refinery§f (primary): §e150mB§f per §e1,000mB§f hot crude oil.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: §e100mB§f Light Oil → §e40mB§f Diesel + §e60mB§f Kerosene.
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f: §e1,000mB§f Light Oil → §e500mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons + §e150mB§f Liquid Hydrogen + §e100mB§f Reformate Gas.
§b• Chemical Plant§f: Combine §e200mB§f Light Oil + §e200mB§f Mercury + §e1×§f Desh Blend → §e1×§f Desh Ingot (one of the only ways to produce desh).
§b• Industrial Solidification Machine§f: §e1,200mB§f Light Oil → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e370mB§f Light Oil → §e1×§f Petroleum Coke + §e37mB§f Coker Gas.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e490mB§f Light Oil → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.
§b• Rotary Furnace§f: §e100mB§f Light Oil + DeshReady™ Blend → §e1×§f Ingot.

§eTip§f: Light Oil is one of the few renewable products (via the §bDesulfurized Crude Oil§f refinery path). It burns efficiently in generators and is critical for desh production — do not waste it by cracking it down unnecessarily!
